name: Beef Stew
instructions:
Heat 1/4 cup of olive oil in a heavy large pot over medium-high heat. Add beef and saute until brown on all sides (about 5 minutes). Add garlic and saute for another minute.

Now add 6 cups of water, guinness, red wine, tomato paste, sugar, thyme, Fish sauce, soy sauce, worchestershire sauce, and the bay leaves and stir it up. Bring it to a boil then reduce the heat to medium-low, then cover and let it simmer for about an hour, give it a few stirs.
 
Now get to work chopping the potatoes, carrots, and onions. Once its all cut up melt about 1/4 a stick of butter in a big pan (since your biggest is simmering get your second biggest). Once the butter is bubbly toss in the potatoes, onion, and carrots. Saute it all up for about 20 minutes (or until its golden).
 
Once the hour of simmering is up toss in the veggies and simmer with the lid off for about 40 minutes. Everything should be nice and soft at this point. Dig out the bay leaves and toss them.
